title: ibervillea fusiformis
intro: Ibervillea fusiformis is a plant species native to the Americas, commonly known as the "Mexican prickly poppy" or "Mexican cleaver", and belongs to the family Cactaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Ibervillea fusiformis has been found to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments. It is often used in herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and has been traditionally used by indigenous communities in Mexico and other parts of the Americas.
